tenant_rules.rule: all tenant-owned tables must include CustomerId
tenant_rules.query_rule: all SQL must explicitly include WHERE CustomerId = @CustomerId

core_model.User: identity boundary — global entity, NOT tenant-scoped per Customer
core_model.Customer: tenant boundary — all data entities partitioned by CustomerId
core_model.Profile: PRIMARY capability + inner data partition boundary — ProfileId is the decisive runtime scope
core_model.ICurrentUser: {UserId, CustomerId, ProfileId, LanguageId, ImpersonateFromUserId} — pure read interface, no mutable state
core_model.JWT_shape: {UserId, CustomerId, ProfileId, ImpersonateFromUserId} — thin, no roles, no LanguageId

hard_rules: profileId=0 is NEVER valid runtime state — CurrentUserMiddleware rejects with HTTP 401
customerId=0 is NEVER valid runtime state — CurrentUserMiddleware rejects with HTTP 401
No handler ever receives ICurrentUser with ProfileId=0 or CustomerId=0
Exactly one ICurrentUser implementation exists (CurrentUserMiddleware). No diverging WebWorkContext variants.
LanguageId is read from User entity in DB at middleware time — NOT baked into JWT
Localization is blocked until IDENTITY_ACCESS_CORE build steps 1–8 are complete
All feature domains that touch auth/access/scoping must align with 000_foundational_analysis.json before implementation begins
jwt_requires_profile_and_customer: true — JWT NEVER issued with ProfileId=0 or CustomerId=0
auto_profile_selection: only when exactly 1 profile accessible
profile_selection_is_terminal: POST /auth/select-profile always issues JWT on success
refresh_reads_db: RefreshTokenHandler reads User.CurrentCustomerId/CurrentProfileId from DB, never from JWT
partial_tokens_restricted: userId-only tokens not accepted by operational handlers
cross_tenant_profile_forbidden: Profile.CustomerId must equal CurrentCustomerId at selection time
